# ADR 0005: Dev Container as the Canonical Development and CI Environment

<!-- Generated by seed-golden-context | Last updated: 2026-05-11 -->

## Status

Accepted — introduced in DX-822 (2026-01-18)

## Context

PHP version matrix compatibility (8.0–8.4), Composer dependency installation, and tool versions (php-cs-fixer, PHPStan) have historically caused "works on my machine" failures. CI used CircleCI-specific Docker images that diverged from local setups. External contributors without PHP installed could not run any checks.

## Decision

Add a `.devcontainer` configuration (Dockerfile + `devcontainer.json`) that provides a fully reproducible PHP dev environment. GitHub Actions CI was migrated to use the same devcontainer via `@devcontainers/cli` — every CI job runs `devcontainer up && devcontainer exec ... <command>`. Local contributors open the repo in VS Code Dev Containers (or use the `devcontainer` CLI) for an identical environment.

Evidence: `.devcontainer/devcontainer.json`, `.devcontainer/Dockerfile`, `.github/workflows/ci.yml` (all jobs use `devcontainer exec`), commit `970f89c` (`chore: add devcontainer contributor workflow [DX-822]`).

## Consequences

- Dev and CI environments are identical — environment-specific failures are eliminated.
- CI jobs are slower due to Docker build overhead on every run (mitigated by layer caching).
- Contributors need Docker installed; pure-PHP setups without Docker cannot run the standard test commands.
- The PHP version for local development defaults to 8.1 (set via `PHP_VERSION` env var in devcontainer args); CI tests the full matrix (8.0–8.4).
